data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the current state. Let me plan the migration from xUnit v2 to v3:

1. **Directory.Packages.props**: Replace v2 packages with v3 packages
   - Remove `xunit` (v2 meta-package)
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (v2 runner)
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (not needed for v3)
   - Add `xunit.v3` (v3 meta-package)
   - Add `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 version OR use `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` etc.
   
   Wait, I need to verify the correct v3 packages. The main package is `xunit.v3` for the core framework, and for running tests with `dotnet test` I'll need the v3 version of `xunit.runner.visualstudio`. Actually, xUnit v3 can run tests directly as executables, which means the test project needs to be configured as an executable output type rather than a class library.

   For the migration, the key changes are setting the output type to executable, swapping the `xunit` package for `xunit.v3`, and removing the test SDK and runner packages since they're not needed in v3. The using statements, attributes, and Assert methods stay largely the same, but the test runner is now built into the assembly itself. I should also update the global.json to use Microsoft.Testing.Platform instead of VSTest, though I'm still working through exactly how that configuration should look. I'm realizing that with xUnit v3, the test project becomes an executable with an auto-generated entry point, so I don't need those extra test SDK packages or runners at all. Let me check NuGet to confirm the latest version details.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.v3:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)
*   [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab)

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

[![Powered by NDepend](https://raw.github.com/xunit/media/main/powered-by-ndepend-transparent.png)](http://www.ndepend.com/)

data.content:
Now I have a clear picture. Latest stable `xunit.v3` is **3.2.2**. Here's the migration plan:

1. **Directory.Packages.props** — replace v2 packages with `xunit.v3`
2. **TestProject.csproj** — add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`, swap package refs
3. **global.json** — switch runner to `Microsoft.Testing.Platform`
4. **BasicTests.cs** — no changes needed (`[Fact]`/`Assert` API is compatible)

arguments.old_str:
<ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
The latest stable version of `xunit.v3` is `3.2.2`. Now let me apply the migration:

1. **Directory.Packages.props**: Replace v2 packages with `xunit.v3` 3.2.2
2. **TestProject.csproj**: 
   - Add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` (v3 tests are executables)
   - Replace package references: remove `xunit`, `xunit.runner.visualstudio`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, add `xunit.v3`
3. **BasicTests.cs**: The code should work as-is since `[Fact]` and `Assert.Equal` are the same


4. **global.json**: I need to update the runner configuration to use `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` since xUnit v3 relies on it instead of the older test runners.

<ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.content:
All tests pass. Here's a summary of the changes:

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| **Directory.Packages.props** | Replaced `xunit` 2.9.3, `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 2.8.2, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` 18.3.0 → `xunit.v3` 3.2.2 |
| **TestProject.csproj** | Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`, single `xunit.v3` package ref |
| **global.json** | Runner changed from `VSTest` → `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` |
| **BasicTests.cs** | No changes needed — `[Fact]` and `Assert` APIs are compatible |
